{"key_event":"Louis-Napoléon’s coup and authoritarian Second Empire","reasoning":"The 1851 coup, centralized imperial constitution, prefectural control, censorship, official candidates, policing, and Haussmann-era urban redesign pushed state capacity and coercive reach from already strong to very strong. Civil society fell sharply as republican-socialist clubs, press, worker networks, and electoral opposition were repressed, though managed male suffrage, Catholic associations, economic modernization, and a small republican opposition kept it from being atomized.","society_change":-2.1,"society_power":5.3,"state_change":1.1,"state_power":8.6}
